growth_records.strain: not stated in text (model Roseobacter-group marine bacterium; metabolic model per ref. 33)
growth_records.medium: Artificial seawater medium ASWb with 1 mM glucose

up.method: sole carbon source (1 mM); consumption not measured as a flux

mfa.note: 50 uM [13C-methyl]DMSP, 2 h; label traced into methionine cycle. Isotope abundance, NOT a flux
Same experiment; MTA is spermidine-synthesis byproduct downstream of SAM

growth_records.cite: Sperfeld, Martin et al. Algal methylated compounds shorten the lag phase of Phaeobacter inhibens bacteria. Nature Microbiology (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-024-01742-6. [PMC11306105]
growth_records.method: reported (Growthcurver logistic fit of OD600 growth curves yields modelled growth rate, lag time and doubling time; numeric mu/td only in figures/source data, not in article text). DMSP supplementation shortened lag by up to 2.5 h without changing doubling time
growth_records.snippet: doubling times were unchanged, as evident from the identical slopes, while lag times were shortened ... 13C-labelled SAM (isotope abundance for M+1: 41.6%) and 13C-labelled MTA (isotope abundance for M+1: 47.9%)
growth_records.notes: Defined-medium (ASWb, full salt composition captured above) batch growth of the prokaryote P. inhibens on 1 mM glucose, 30 C, aerobic. Growth quantified by Growthcurver (mu, lag, doubling time) but numeric mu/td are in source-data/figures, not article text, so growth_rate_per_h/doubling_time_h left null. No mmol/gDW/h substrate-uptake or product-secretion rates reported. 13C-methyl DMSP labelling of SAM (41.6%) and MTA (47.9%) are isotope abundances (M+1), not MFA fluxes. Cellular C1 requirement estimate (190.1 amol C1 and 23,800 amol ATP per cell duplication) is a model-based calculation adapted from E. coli (ref. 32) and the P. inhibens metabolic model (ref. 33), not a measured rate.

growth_records.medium: defined marine medium with mixture of 11 organic C sources (3 sugars + 8 amino acids)

up.units: 1/h (specific uptake rate, model-inferred during inhibition by mannitol/N-acetylglucosamine)
up.method: DEB model fit to depletion curves

yields.note: theoretical ATP yield per substrate computed by Wunsch et al. (SI Table S1); N-acetylglucosamine highest, glucose lowest; numeric values not in main text

growth_records.cite: Lucken, Leonhard et al. A network-based analysis of bacterial growth on substrate mixtures uncovers glucose inhibition in Phaeobacter inhibens. Scientific Reports (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41598-025-33583-6. [PMC12791127]
growth_records.snippet: P. inhibens continuously consumed all amino acids (initially 1.3 mM each) without diauxic shifts or inhibition patterns ... mannitol and N-acetylglucosamine were depleted much faster than the other substrates ... effectively blocking signif
growth_records.notes: P. inhibens DSM 17395, aerobic batch on 11-substrate mixture. This paper is a RE-ANALYSIS of experimental growth curves originally from Wunsch et al. (ref 21); the raw measured substrate uptake rates, per-substrate growth rates (mu), BGE and ATP yields are shown only in figures (Fig 1a, Fig 2c) and SI, and are NOT tabulated numerically in the main text. Only concrete in-text concentration: each of the 8 amino acids initially 1.3 mM. Glucose specific uptake <0.1/h is a DEB-model-derived value (not a direct measurement), flagged is_experimental:false. Temperature and pH stated as controlled but numeric values not given in text. Cited source of experimental data: Wunsch et al. (ref 21).

phenotype_groups.label: Complex/peptone base (acid-production test)
phenotype_groups.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
